purpose: Trigger routing hints for host agents and installers. These terms do not replace the SKILL.md description; they reduce ambiguity across multi-skill environments.
strong_trigger_keywords: accept minor revision major revision reject

routing_rules.condition: The user asks whether a manuscript, preprint, proposal, figure set, dataset, proof, model, code artifact, or central scientific claim is convincing, publishable, novel, or sufficiently supported.
The user asks to rewrite or audit a biological protocol, SOP, bench note, or wet-lab workflow for execution readiness.
routing_rules.action: Route to biological-protocol-reviewer if installed; otherwise state that this skill can only review the scientific claim and protocol-derived evidence, not generate a full SOP.

routing_rules.condition: The user asks only for language polishing, figure styling, reviewer response drafting, citation formatting, or paper-to-PPT conversion.
routing_rules.action: Do not trigger rigorous-reviewer unless the user explicitly asks for scientific critique or claim stress-testing.

routing_rules.condition: The user asks only for paper lookup, database lookup, citation graph search, or public accession verification.
routing_rules.action: Use lookup/database companion skills if installed; trigger rigorous-reviewer only for synthesis and final scientific judgment.

activation_examples: Use Rigorous-Reviewer to review this manuscript.
这个文章如果做出来能发哪个期刊？
Does this central claim have decisive evidence?
Find the hidden loopholes and alternative explanations.
Audit this paper for novelty, reproducibility, statistics, and benchmark integrity.
